hero.intro: I build software with a focus on AI, automation and modern digital products. From RAG pipelines and reinforcement-learning agents to web and mobile apps, I build systems that don't just work technically but also serve a clear purpose.
hero.intro2: My focus is on clean architecture, well-thought-out workflows and the meaningful use of AI in the development process. To me, good software today no longer comes from more code alone, but from the right combination of structure, product understanding and intelligent tools.

about.heading: About
about.p1Pre: I'm a software developer from Bavaria, currently doing my M.Sc. in Computer Science with a focus on Artificial Intelligence at Goethe University Frankfurt. Alongside my studies I'm building
about.p1Post: — a mobile app for beauty bookings that I built from the ground up.
about.p2: I work with AI-assisted tools like Claude, Cursor and Copilot every day — as an amplifier, not a replacement for understanding. The value comes from clean architecture and clear requirements, not from the prompt alone.
about.p3: I'm looking for an environment where AI is applied in practice and where real systems are built around real requirements.

items.title: Working Student, Software Development

items.title: Bachelor of Science — Software Design
items.description: Focus on Data Science. Thesis: reinforcement learning for adaptive traffic control (Python, SUMO, PPO).

chatbot.title: Portfolio Chatbot
chatbot.short: RAG pipeline: Gemini embeddings, NumPy vector search, Flask serverless
chatbot.full: Full RAG pipeline: user query → Gemini Embedding 001 (RETRIEVAL_QUERY) → cosine similarity via NumPy → top-3 chunks → Gemini 2.5 Flash with a guardrail system prompt. A deliberate decision against an external vector database: the index is built offline from profile.md and committed to the repo as JSON — loaded into a normalized NumPy matrix at cold start and cached at module level. Deployed as a Flask WSGI serverless function on Vercel — no persistent server, no running costs.
notes: No vector-DB server: precomputed JSON, loaded as a normalized NumPy matrix at cold start & cached at module level (warm invocations)
Cosine similarity = dot product of normalized vectors — a single NumPy matmul
task_type RETRIEVAL_DOCUMENT (index) vs. RETRIEVAL_QUERY (question) for better retrieval quality
Guardrail in the system prompt: playfully declines off-topic questions, answers in the language of the question

rl-traffic.title: RL Traffic Control
rl-traffic.short: Weather-aware MaskablePPO agent for adaptive traffic-light control in SUMO
rl-traffic.full: Bachelor's thesis: a reinforcement-learning agent controls a 4-phase traffic light (junction J13) in the SUMO simulator via the TraCI API. Trained with MaskablePPO (sb3-contrib): an ActionMasker hides invalid phase switches, a TrafficLightManager enforces min-green, yellow and red-yellow phases. Curriculum learning across 4 levels (HDF5-backed, automatic level-up) and a weather-aware model: real Open-Meteo data (rain/snow/fog) is translated into SUMO vehicle parameters via an empirical friction and visibility model. Hyperparameter search via Optuna, vectorized parallel simulation (SubprocVecEnv), TensorBoard logging and checkpoint resume.
notes: MaskablePPO instead of vanilla PPO: the ActionMasker masks invalid actions (e.g. holding a phase during a yellow transition)
Weather-aware: real Open-Meteo JSONs → empirical friction/visibility model → setMaxSpeed/Accel/Tau/MinGap per vehicle
Curriculum over 4 levels with HDF5 performance tracking; level-up tied to reward, wait, slope, violation & teleport rate
Reward combines waiting time, Δ waiting time, queue, fairness (std across groups), teleport and violation penalties — weights scale per level
SubprocVecEnv for parallel SUMO instances, Optuna HPO, automatic checkpoint resume

beautify.title: Beautify
beautify.short: Escrow marketplace for beauty bookings — Expo, Supabase, Stripe Connect
beautify.full: My own startup: a mobile marketplace for beauty services with escrow payments. A single React Native codebase on Expo runs on iOS and Android. The backend is Supabase (Postgres, Auth, Row Level Security); payments go through Stripe Connect as escrow: the amount is held at booking time and only paid out to the provider after the service is delivered. Solo-driven from MVP to launch — product, tech and business in one person.
notes: Escrow: the amount is authorized/held at booking and only paid out to the provider after confirmed service (Stripe Connect)
One Expo/React Native codebase → iOS + Android from a single source
Supabase as the backend: Postgres + Auth + Row Level Security; Stripe calls run server-side via Edge Functions (secrets stay safe)
Stripe Connect manages provider accounts & marketplace payouts

cicd.title: CI/CD Vulnerability Detection
cicd.short: GitLab-CI DevSecOps: SAST/SCA/DAST against 2 apps → Angular vuln dashboard
cicd.full: DevSecOps pipelines (GitLab CI) for two applications: the intentionally vulnerable OWASP Juice Shop (Node.js) and a ToDo-list app from the professors (Java/Maven). On every push the code runs through the same security gates: Semgrep (SAST), Gitleaks (secret scan), OWASP Dependency-Check (SCA of dependencies), Trivy (container/filesystem scan) and OWASP ZAP (DAST against the deployed container). Each stage exports JSON/SARIF reports, which a self-built Angular dashboard aggregates: number of vulnerabilities, type and name (incl. ID) and — where available — a link to the fix. Scheduled pipelines (cron) additionally clear the caches (global + Dependency-Check).
notes: Two targets, same pipeline: OWASP Juice Shop (Node.js) + the professors' ToDo list (Java/Maven)
Security gates: Semgrep (SAST) · Gitleaks (secrets) · OWASP Dependency-Check (SCA) · Trivy (container/FS) · OWASP ZAP (DAST)
Stages: build → test → SAST → SCA → publish (Docker) → container scan → DAST → deploy
Scheduled pipeline (cron, rules: schedule) clears the global & Dependency-Check caches; for the ToDo app the DAST scan also ran on a schedule
All reports (JSON/SARIF) → Angular dashboard: count, vulnerability name + ID, link to the fix

rl-slotcar.title: RL Slot-Car
rl-slotcar.short: Autonomous slot car: top-down webcam + OpenCV tracking → DQN/PPO → digital pot
rl-slotcar.full: An RL agent drives a real slot car autonomously. A top-down webcam films the track; image processing (OpenCV) converts each frame to black & white and keeps only green — so the green-painted car is reliably detected. As a first step the car drives frame by frame to map out the track. The agent then outputs a continuous speed value between 0 and 100, which regulates the motor speed via a digital potentiometer. Autonomous driving was trained first with Deep Q-Learning, then with a PPO approach.
notes: Top-down webcam + OpenCV: each frame is converted to B/W and only green is kept → the green-painted car is unambiguously trackable
Step 1: the car drives frame by frame → the track is mapped (environment setup)
Agent output = continuous speed value 0–100, regulated via a digital potentiometer (hardware-in-the-loop)
Autonomous driving trained first with Deep Q-Learning, then with a PPO approach

idle-game.title: Idle City Game
idle-game.short: Cookie-Clicker-style idle game in Unity/C#: Eco-Points → city upgrades on a tilemap
idle-game.full: Idle game (Cookie-Clicker genre) in Unity/C#: with the digital currency "Eco-Points" you buy upgrades that grow a city rebuilt as a tilemap (Aschaffenburg) — trees, flowers, charging stations, bike lanes. Deliberately object-oriented & data-driven: each upgrade is an UpgradeObject instance (cost, eco/s, level, allowed tilemap areas), so new upgrades need no code change. EcoPoints generates the currency per second (the idle mechanic), PrestigeSystem grants a reset multiplier, GameManager saves/loads and computes offline earnings. UI built with Unity UI Toolkit, assets hand-drawn. Created in a software-engineering project with full requirements engineering & user testing.
notes: Eco-Points = digital currency: EcoPoints generates it per second (idle mechanic) and settles purchases (ApplyUpgrade/SpendEcoPoints)
Object-oriented & dynamic: each upgrade is an UpgradeObject instance (cost, eco/s, level, allowed tilemap bounds) — new upgrades without code changes
Main places the upgrade tiles via vector bounds (randomly within allowed areas) on the tilemap — the city grows visibly
PrestigeSystem: reset for a permanent multiplier; GameManager saves/loads and computes offline earnings
UI with Unity UI Toolkit (UIDocument); buttons grey = unaffordable, blue = affordable; hand-drawn cartoon assets
